title: Data from: Targeted re-sequencing of five Douglas-fir provenances reveals population structure and putative target genes of positive selection
abstract: Douglas-fir (Pseudotsuga menziesii) occurs in a coastal and an interior variety that differ in drought tolerance and other adaptive traits. To characterize genome-wide levels of genetic diversity in coding regions and to identify genes involved in local adaptation, we used targeted sequence capture to re-sequence 72 trees representing one interior and four coastal provenances. A custom NimbleGen sequence capture array was designed from 57,110 putative unique transcripts (PUTs) to enrich genomic sequencing libraries for these regions. Sequence analysis revealed that almost 100 % of target regions were captured and sequenced in at least one individual. We found 79,910 single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) whose genotypes were called in all individuals. The data confirmed genetic differentiation between interior and coastal provenances and revealed little differentiation between coastal provenances. The nucleotide diversity of the total sample was estimated as π=0.0032, which is at the lower end of values observed in conifers. Outlier tests of genetic differentiation identified 58 high-confidence candidate genes for directional selection with a broad functional diversity. A priori defined genes involved in drought tolerance showed a significantly higher genetic differentiation between interior and coastal Douglas-fir suggesting a different evolution despite a low level of polymorphism. The observed data showed a reduced level of polymorphisms with low minor allele frequencies compared to standard demographic models with two populations and migration. Targeted sequence capture is an efficient method to characterize the genetic diversity of conifer trees with a complex genome.
usageNotes:
README

This is a README file describing the files in repository.

COMMANDS

This file contains a detailed description on how to perform the analysis.

Admixture results

Archive containing example Admixture results for K = 2 to K = 7.
admixture.tar.gz

bash_scripts

Archives with bash scripts used in data analysis. See README.txt and COMMANDS.txt.

bayenv_FIVEPOPS

Example bayenv result.

bayenv_TWOPOPS

Example bayenv result.

bayescan

Example results of bayescan.

doug_SeqCapExp.dump

psql database dump, see README.txt and COMMANDS.txt.

Results

Several files used for and generated during data analysis, for instance a .vcf file. See REAMDE.txt and COMMANDS.txt
filtered_e1_results.tar.gz

lositan

Example lositan results

msABC

msABC files used in the publication.

population_files

Archive with files containing population information, e.g. needed for Fst.

python_scripts

Archive of python scripts used in data analysis. See Readme in that archive and COMMANDS.txt

ref2.bed

ref2.bed files needed for coverage calculations. See COMMANDS.txt

Rscripts

Archive with R-scripts used during data analysis. See Readme in that archive and COMMANDS.txt

samples_sorted_by_locID

File with information about sample and location IDs.

target_regions

This archive contains the target regions as well as results of blastx vs TAIR and Ptaeda. Furthermore it contains bwa index files.
